mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs compress corporate margins and reduce the present value of future earnings via a higher discount rate, leading to negative equity market performance over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.12% is the highest since 2007, indicating a restrictive policy stance.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) during periods when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained, strong growth in nonfarm payrolls signals an overheating labor market. This fuels wage-push inflation concerns, forcing the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance, which acts as a headwind for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent payroll reports in 2023 have consistently shown resilient job growth despite rate hikes.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is not followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing retail sales growth and elevated inflation creates a stagflationary pressure. This dynamic squeezes corporate profit margins as revenues stagnate while input costs remain high, leading to negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL > 4% AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation remains above the Fed's target while some consumer spending metrics show signs of slowing.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production is a leading indicator of a slowdown in the goods-producing sector and broader economic activity. This decline signals falling corporate revenues and earnings, prompting a repricing of equities to the downside.
evidence_refs: fact:Manufacturing PMIs have been in contractionary territory for several months leading up to August 2023.
failure_condition: The information coefficient (IC) of the signal's 3-month change relative to 20-day forward returns is positive over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its cycle low is a robust, non-linear indicator of a recession. This signal of sharp labor market deterioration causes investors to de-risk portfolios, leading to significant equity market drawdowns.
evidence_refs: fact:The unemployment rate remains historically low at 3.5%, so this signal is currently inactive.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth is the definitive signal of an expanding economy. This fundamental backdrop supports aggregate corporate earnings growth and justifies higher equity valuations, leading to positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The most recent GDP print showed positive growth, defying some recession forecasts.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A central bank raising interest rates while the housing market is actively contracting signals a high risk of a policy error. This combination of tightening financial conditions and a weakening key economic sector is acutely negative for investor sentiment and future growth.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0.25 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has been hiking rates aggressively in 2022-2023 while housing activity has slowed considerably from its peak.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of the months where this trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in month-over-month retail sales growth provides a high-frequency signal of resilient consumer demand. This positively surprises markets, leading to upward revisions in near-term earnings estimates and improved sentiment, driving short-term positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending has been a key support for the economy throughout the current cycle.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting the direction of 5-day forward returns is not statistically distinguishable from 50% (binomial test p-value > 0.10).
